key_themes: Big Tech earnings wave drives record closes — S&P 500 tops 7,200 for first time
AI infrastructure capex debate: cloud beats impress but massive spending spooks Meta and Microsoft investors
Earnings season strength broad-based across industrials, healthcare, and consumer sectors

stocks.reason: Q1 2026 earnings beat: fastest cloud growth on record; stock surged ~10%, the largest single-day gain since April 2025; drove Nasdaq and S&P 500 to new closing records

Q2 FY2026 earnings beat (EPS $2.65 vs $2.55 est); stock surged ~16% — best day in months — on record automotive revenue exceeding $5B annualized and CEO comments on China inventory recovery and new hyperscaler data center chip deal
After-hours Q2 2026 earnings: $111.2B revenue (up 17% YoY), record-breaking quarter; EPS $2.01 beat $1.94 estimate; $100B additional buyback authorized; iPhone 17 family cited as most popular ever

Q1 2026 earnings beat: EPS $5.54 vs $4.64 estimate (+$0.90 beat); stock popped ~10%; revenue grew 22% YoY; company raised full-year revenue outlook as power equipment business benefits from AI infrastructure surge

Fell 8.6% despite Q1 revenue beat; JPMorgan downgraded to Neutral (PT cut $825→$725) citing sky-high AI capex and lack of visibility into AI product pipeline; disappointing user growth added to pressure
After-hours crash of ~21%: slashed full-year bookings guidance to $7.33B–$7.60B from prior $8.28B–$8.55B — a dramatic cut that shocked the market
After-hours +7%: Q1 revenue surged 69% YoY to $663M and Daily Active Uniques rose 17% YoY to 126.8M, both beating consensus estimates
After-hours +8%: Q2 results easily beat consensus and guidance topped views in a beat-and-raise report
Q1 2026 earnings and revenue blew past analyst expectations; stock rose ~7-9%, one of the top performers in the S&P 500 today
Stock exploded ~17% (from $71.13 to $83.49) after Q1 2026 EPS of $1.71 beat $1.67 estimate on $2.33B revenue; company raised full-year 2026 diluted EPS guidance to $5.32–$5.48
Q1 same-store sales rose 0.5%, crushing analyst consensus that had expected a 0.7% decline; stock rose ~4% on the reversal of bearish sentiment heading into the print
Q1 2026 earnings beat: $16.29B revenue up 5% YoY; Keytruda cancer therapy revenue topped $8.03B (up 12%); company raised adjusted EPS outlook to $5.04–$5.16; stock up ~5%
Fell 3.9% despite cloud revenue beat; $190B capex guidance for AI infrastructure raised concerns about spending discipline; weighed on Big Tech sentiment alongside Meta
Q3 fiscal 2026 results impressed with strong performance surpassing market expectations across key metrics; part of broader semiconductor strength day
Oppenheimer analyst Param Singh initiated coverage with an Outperform rating and $200 price target, one of the highest on the Street; bullish initiation drew significant attention
